findings.name: vision_overlap
findings.evidence: In the quality-vs-cost chart, the x-axis title overlaps the tick labels around 15–20%, making the axis labeling harder to read.

findings.name: vision_overlap
findings.evidence: In the mobile chart, the x-axis title overlaps the tick labels and baseline labels near the bottom of the plot.

findings.name: vision_overlap
findings.evidence: In the visible quality-vs-cost chart, the x-axis title overlaps the tick labels near the bottom of the plot.

findings.name: vision_clipped_content
findings.evidence: Mobile render has page-level horizontal overflow: a horizontal scrollbar is visible, the top 'Files: ...' badge is cut off at the right edge, and multiple code blocks extend beyond the viewport. Main prose remains readable, so this is a warning rather than a fail.

findings.name: vision_clipped_content
findings.evidence: Mobile viewport shows page-level horizontal overflow with a horizontal scrollbar; the 'Files: scripts/check_candle_renderings.py + 2 new fi...' badge is clipped offscreen and the first code block extends beyond the visible area. Main content is still mostly readable.

findings.name: vision_chart_malformed
findings.evidence: In the 'Artifact Progress (Latest Week)' panel, the mobile rendering shows each progress row as a mostly blank card with only the label and percentage; the green horizontal progress bars visible in the desktop rendering are missing, making the chart visually incomplete/misleading.
